title: Taxi in China Without Chinese
metaDescription: A concise, practical guide for navigating taxis in China without speaking Mandarin, covering key phrases and apps.

sections.heading: Getting Started
sections.content: Prepare a translation app like Baidu Translate or Google Translate with the offline Chinese package. Always have your destination written in Chinese characters on your phone or a card. Ask your hotel to write your destination and a return address on a card in Chinese.

sections.heading: Key Tips
sections.content: Use the built-in meter; politely insist if the driver suggests a fixed price. Use a navigation app like Baidu Maps or Amap (Gaode) to follow the route in real-time. Have your payment ready; most taxis accept WeChat Pay, Alipay, or cash.

sections.heading: What to Remember
sections.content: Note the taxi's number and take a photo of it for lost items or complaints. Consider using the DiDi (similar to Uber) app for easier communication and upfront pricing. Learn 'Qù zhège dìzhǐ' (去这个地址) meaning 'Go to this address.'

keyTakeaways: Prepare destination cards in Chinese characters before your trip.
Use ride-hailing apps like DiDi for transparency and easier communication.
Always ensure the meter is running and have mobile payment ready.
relatedPhrases: Dǎ chē (打车) - To hail a taxi
